metadata.notes: Corrected after the 2026-07-15 rewind record: Kiro sidecars can carry a top-level parent_session_id, disproving the former claim that no parent field exists at any depth. The current adapter still tails JSONL and uses sidecars only for cwd, so it cannot project a spawned child edge as ParentSessionID; this cell remains frozen pending a real /spawn trace plus adapter support.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable
driver_capability: ready

Kiro exposes /spawn and subagent tools, so the agent supports the background-agent primitive. The old assessment was too broad: a live /rewind child sidecar in Kiro 2.6.0 contains top-level parent_session_id, proving this storage schema can persist an edge. That does not prove a /spawn child has the same edge.

The daemon remains incapable today because the Kiro adapter tails only *.jsonl and reads the sibling .json sidecar solely to obtain cwd; neither the parser nor session discovery projects parent_session_id into ParentSessionID. Thus even an observed /spawn edge would currently surface as an unlinked top-level session.

## Unblock

Capture a real /spawn parent/child pair and teach the adapter's sidecar path to emit the verified parent_session_id as ParentSessionID. Then re-assess this cell; do not infer /spawn semantics from the rewind record alone.
caveats: The rewind child is not evidence that every Kiro child type, including /spawn, writes parent_session_id.
The prior absolute 'NO parent field at any depth' claim was false and has been removed.
The driver can run the needed send, wait_turn, and sleep steps once daemon observability is available.

sources.note: Live rewind child sidecar with top-level parent_session_id.
Sidecar is used for cwd discovery only; no parent projection.

recipe.frozen_reason: Await a real /spawn trace and adapter support for projecting a verified sidecar parent_session_id.
